notes: Gravity for THE BACK ROOM CALLS. The hardest scenario — the high-stakes table on the lower deck is in session and the signal pulls on the floor. Paul reaches by phone. The back room door has been closed for forty minutes. Pressing DOUBT and STAGNATION hard; the BLOOM still has to grow but the room is fighting it. The third kicks at irregular intervals. The threshold is louder this Friday than it has been any Friday in the year.

deck.title: the back room door is closed
deck.flavor: it has been closed for forty minutes. somebody is winning. you pass the back corridor between seatings. the door is closed the way the door is closed.

deck.title: the card room lets a laugh out
deck.flavor: from down the back corridor. one man's laugh. you do not, this week, know him. the laugh stops at half a beat. somebody has shushed him.

deck.title: paul calls at 9:14
deck.flavor: the stand line. sammy routes it back to you. mr. paul. he confirms sunday's reservation. he asks, in passing, who is at the boat tonight.

deck.title: the lower stagnation pulse
deck.flavor: you feel it in the floor of the dining room. a long slow under-frequency that is, you have come to suspect, the back room's gravity expressing itself through the hull.

deck.title: the third kicks irregular
deck.flavor: not on her usual rhythm. once at 8:42. once at 8:43. then nothing for forty minutes. then once at 9:23. you log it in the small private accounting.

deck.title: a chair scrapes below
deck.flavor: you hear it through the floor. somebody at the back-room table has, briefly, stood up. they sit back down. the scraping was the only sign.

deck.title: the helm light stays on late
deck.flavor: dante has not, this Friday, done his rounds. the leaded window glows steady. you see his shape sitting at the desk. he is on the phone.

deck.title: antonio doesn't call at nine
deck.flavor: the once-a-friday call does not, this friday, come. you check the phone. you have not, this evening, missed it.

deck.title: alberto arrives with the briefcase
deck.flavor: he has, in three years, never brought a briefcase to the boat. tonight he is carrying one. he does not stop at the stand. he goes up the stair to the helm.

deck.title: sammy pours a glass for someone not at the bar
deck.flavor: you see him at the well. he fills a rocks glass. neat. he sets it on the corner of the bar. nobody is sitting there. he does not, today, look at you.

deck.title: gloria does not pass the water
deck.flavor: she has, three times this year, set the glass on your stand without looking. tonight is not the night. she takes her tables. she does not look toward you. the not-looking is the saying.

deck.title: the back room door opens briefly
deck.flavor: from down the corridor. a slice of warmer-than-the-corridor light. a man you do not recognize comes out. he passes the catering office. he goes up to the main floor. he leaves by the gangway.

deck.title: halsey drops off with the pickup outside
deck.flavor: cole at the back. he brings the figs. he says, on the way out: that pickup is back. louisiana plates. been there since wednesday.

deck.title: aria speaks clearly at the stand
deck.flavor: for the first time tonight, sentence-shaped. she says, in your head: he asked about you. you understand, immediately, who she means. the back room. you take the breath at the stand. you do not, this turn, answer her aloud.

deck.title: the back room settles
deck.flavor: the door, by 11:48, opens. the men file out one by one through the staff exit. you do not, this Friday, get a look at the last face. the boat exhales. the floor is, briefly, yours.
